task.title: Installing DITA-OT
task.shortdesc: Download, install, and verify DITA Open Toolkit 4.x on your machine.

p.b: After completing this chapter, you will be able to:

ul.li: Install DITA-OT 4.x on Windows, macOS, or Linux
Java 17 or later — DITA-OT 4.x requires Java 17+. Check with java -version.
500 MB free disk space for the toolkit and build artifacts.
Network access to download the release archive.

context.p: DITA-OT is a free, open-source publishing engine that transforms DITA XML into HTML5, PDF, and other formats. It is the standard toolchain for DITA publishing.

step.cmd: Verify Java is installed and meets the minimum version.

info.p: You should see output like openjdk version "17.0.x" or higher. If Java is not installed or is too old:
Visit dita-ot.org/download and download the zip archive, or use the command line:
info.codeblock: # Replace 4.3.5 with the latest version curl -LO https://github.com/dita-ot/dita-ot/releases/download/4.3.5/dita-ot-4.3.5.zip

step.cmd: Extract the archive to a permanent location.

info.codeblock:
# Linux / macOS unzip dita-ot-4.3.5.zip -d /opt/ # The toolkit is now at /opt/dita-ot-4.3.5/
# Windows (PowerShell) Expand-Archive dita-ot-4.3.5.zip -DestinationPath C:\dita-ot

step.cmd: Add the DITA-OT bin/ directory to your PATH.

info.codeblock:
# Linux / macOS — add to ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc export DITA_HOME=/opt/dita-ot-4.3.5 export PATH="$DITA_HOME/bin:$PATH"
# Apply immediately source ~/.bashrc

info.p: Also list installed plugins to confirm a working installation:
You should see a list including org.dita.html5, org.dita.pdf2, and others.

step.cmd: Run a test build using the included samples.

info.codeblock: # Build the included sample to HTML5 dita -i $DITA_HOME/docsrc/samples/sequence.ditamap -f html5 -o /tmp/dita-test-output
info.p: Open /tmp/dita-test-output/index.html in a browser to verify the output.

result.p: DITA-OT is installed and working. You can now build any DITA map into HTML5 or PDF output.

ul.li: dita: command not found — Ensure $DITA_HOME/bin is on your PATH and restart your terminal.
JAVA_HOME is not set — Set JAVA_HOME to your JDK installation directory.
Unsupported class file major version — Your Java version is too old. DITA-OT 4.x requires Java 17+.
Build succeeds but output is empty — Check that your map file path is correct and the map references valid topics.

postreq.p: Next step: Set up your authoring tools, then move on to Authoring DITA Topics.
